Identify three major geopolitical consequences of shifting towards renewable energy sources for oil-dependent countries. The three major geopolitical consequences of shifting towards renewable energy are as follows: 1. Economic instability: Countries that rely entirely on crude oil exports for their economy will face a sharp drop in revenue, which may cripple their domestic budgets and social welfare programs. 2. Decrease in geopolitical influence: The dominance of oil producing countries on the global stage will decrease as the world's attention for energy security shifts away from fossil fuels to new technologies and minerals. 3. Political unrest: The decrease in oil revenues may lead to increased anti-government protests and economic recession in these countries, which may increase the risk of political instability.

The invention of the printing press in the sixteenth century completely changed the way knowledge spread in Europe, challenging the centuries-old social stratification. Before printing, books were handwritten and were restricted to the aristocracy or clergy of the church, maintaining their monopoly on education and power. The arrival of the press reduced the cost of books and made them accessible to the general public. This resulted in the spread of literacy and the rise of the middle and upper classes, which began to question the ideological dominance of the communist church and the modern social order.

Completely disconnected from work: Have a clear policy of turning off laptops and not checking emails or messages once your shift is over. 3. Flexibility: Because the team is in different time zones, prioritize 'asynchronous' (not face-to-face) communication to get the job done. 4. Focus on health: Encourage regular breaks and time for physical activity. 5. Clear goals: Work success is measured not by hours, but by results and deadlines so that there is no pressure on the team.

The clock will be running at 7:24 p.m. Logic: There are a total of 12 hours from 8:00 a.m. to 8:00 p.m. In 12 hours, the clock will move back 12 times 3 = 36 minutes. Subtracting 36 minutes from 8:00 p.m. gives a time of 7:24.

The length of a rectangle is twice its width. It measures 36 meters. What is the length and width of the rectangle? Answer with step-by-step reasoning. The width is 6 meters and the length is 12 meters.

If 5 machines take 5 minutes to make 5 objects, how long will it take 100 machines to make 100 objects, assuming that each machine works at the same speed? Answer with step-by-step reasoning. 5 minutes. Logic: If 5 machines make 5 objects in 5 minutes, this means that one machine takes 5 minutes to make one object. So, if 100 machines work together, they will all make 100 objects at the same time, because each machine will finish its object in 5 minutes.

What is the sum of all even numbers from 2 to 50? Explain your logic step by step. 650. Even numbers up to 2, 4, 6, ..., 50 form a parallel series. Number of terms = 50 ÷ 2 = 25. Sum = (number of terms) × (first term + last term) ÷ 2 = 25 × (2 + 50) ÷ 2 = 25 × 26 = 650.

Write a summary in one sentence: A district in South India has started distributing solar-powered lanterns in households where electricity is not available. Each lantern gets charged during the day and lights up for about eight hours at night. School officials say that children in these households are now able to study after sunset and attendance in evening literacy classes has increased.

Summarize in one sentence: A cooperative of small farmers in a rural area has begun to share the same set of modern harvesting machines instead of buying separate machines by each family. By pooling their money and timing equipment for different fields, farmers have cut their personal costs by almost half. This arrangement has also enabled poor families who were not able to afford such machines alone to harvest their crops on time.

Summarize in one sentence: Public health workers have found that a simple mixture of clean water, salt and sugar can save the lives of children suffering from severe dehydration due to diarrhea. This solution, which can be prepared at home, helps the body absorb fluids much more effectively than water alone. Teaching mothers how to make it correctly has greatly reduced infant mortality in areas where access to hospitals is limited.

Write a summary in one sentence: A coastal city, which used to dump its household waste in open pits, has started a program to separate the waste as wet and dry at source. Now food waste is being converted into compost for local gardens, while plastics and metals are being collected for recycling. Within a year, the amount of waste sent to pits has decreased by more than sixty percent.

Summarize in one sentence: Researchers studying sleep have found that people who maintain a set bedtime even on weekends are more likely to fall asleep early and feel more refreshed when they wake up. The body's internal clock relies on regular signals of light and dark, and irregular routines can weaken these signals. Volunteers who kept their bedtime stable for a month reported better mood and greater concentration during the day.

Summarize in one sentence: A group of villagers revived a dry pond, which once supplied water to their fields. By clearing the sludge and creating small soil barriers to store rainwater, they replenished the pond during the monsoon. This raised the groundwater level and revived the surrounding wells, allowing farmers to grow a second crop in a year.

Write a summary in one sentence: A national program has started to train youths in villages to repair mobile phones and small electronic devices. The three-month course teaches basic circuits, equipment use and customer service. Many graduates have opened small repair shops in their own communities, providing a service that previously required long trips to the city. A training program teaching village youth to repair phones and electronics has enabled graduates to open shops locally, relieving residents of long commutes to the city.

Summarize in one sentence: Scientists have found that some species of fungi partner with tree roots and exchange nutrients through underground networks. Fungi help trees absorb water and minerals from the soil, while trees provide the sugars formed during photosynthesis to fungi. This cooperation enables forests to share resources and cope with drought and disease stress.

Write a summary in one sentence: A city hospital has introduced a messaging system that reminds patients of the timing of their medication and follow-up appointments. Many patients with chronic illnesses would forget to take a dose of medication or go for a check-up earlier, worsening their condition. The hospital reports that since the start of the reminders, significantly more patients are completing their scheduled treatment. Messages sent by the hospital for medication and appointments have helped many patients struggling with chronic illnesses to complete their treatment as scheduled.

The trees also act as barriers that reduce the noise of passing vehicles and help keep the surrounding air cool during the hot months. Officials say the greenery has made roadside resting spots more comfortable and improved air quality in nearby settlements. Planting trees on the side of highways has reduced dust and noise, cooled the air, and improved comfort and air quality for commuters and nearby residents.

Summarize in one sentence: A team of engineers has designed a small and inexpensive sensor that sends alerts to mobile phones when the water tank is empty. In areas where water comes in only a few times a week, families often waste water or miss supplies. Early users of this device say they are now able to manage their limited water supply much more carefully.

For many families, free meals are a big reason to send their children to school regularly. Teachers observed that students are more attentive in the afternoon and get sick less often. One study found that free and nutritious lunches at the school level improve children's attendance, concentration, health and academic performance, as well as lower dropout rates.

Summarize in one sentence: Beekeepers in an agricultural area have observed that keeping beehives near orchards increases the yield of fruit trees. As the bees come and go between the flowers to collect cumin, they carry pollen that fertilizes the flowers. Farmers who let the beekeepers into their land harvested more fruit, while the beekeepers received a steady source of honey.

A community library that lends books as well as tools, sewing machines and sporting goods is helping residents, especially low-income families, to share resources instead of buying less-used items.

Astronomers have detected a distant planet orbiting a star like our Sun. The planet is located at a distance from its star where the temperature on the surface can allow liquid water to exist. Although it is not yet possible to go there, scientists say that studying its atmosphere can reveal whether conditions favorable to life exist there.

Summarize in one sentence: A women's self-help group in a rural area has created a mutual fund by combining monthly savings. Members can borrow from this fund for starting small businesses or for emergency needs at low interest, which has gotten rid of them from lenders charging higher interest. In three years, many women have opened tailor shops and vegetable stalls, and the group's savings have steadily increased.

Summarize in one sentence: A new mobile application allows farmers to take a picture of a leaf of a diseased plant and get immediate suggestions for possible disease and treatment. The app works by comparing the image with a large collection of known plant diseases stored in the phone. Farmers who do not have easy access to agricultural experts can now quickly identify problems and protect their crops.

Summary in one sentence: Health officials have launched a campaign to vaccinate cattle against a disease that is spreading rapidly in livestock and can devastate the herd of farmers. Teams are going to villages to administer free vaccination and teaching farmers to identify the early symptoms of the disease. Since the campaign started, cases of disease have decreased and milk production in the region has become more stable.

Summary in one sentence: The Central Government of India launched a programme to distribute free solar-powered water pumps to small farmers in three drought affected states. These pumps extract groundwater at no fuel cost, allowing farmers to irrigate their crops even in the dry season. In the first year, the participating farmers recorded a 40 per cent increase in their crop yield compared to farmers using traditional diesel pumps.

Summarize in one sentence: Malaria cases in sub-Saharan Africa have fallen by 30 percent in the past decade, largely due to the widespread distribution of insecticide-treated mosquito nets. International health organizations distributed more than 50 million mosquito nets to families in high-risk areas. The greatest decrease in malaria mortality was recorded in children, with a 40 percent decrease in deaths in children under five years of age. Health officials attribute this to the regular use of mosquito nets and community education campaigns. The widespread distribution of insecticide-treated mosquito nets has helped reduce malaria cases by 30 percent in a decade in sub-Saharan Africa, which has seen the greatest decrease in mortality in children under five years of age.

As the glaciers retreat, the flow of rivers is expected to become uncertain, threatening both agricultural and urban water supplies. Researchers have warned that if greenhouse gas emissions are not significantly reduced, many small glaciers could disappear altogether by 2100. Since 1970, the Himalayan glaciers have lost nearly 40% of their ice, threatening the rapid flow of water supplies to 20 million South Asians.

Summarize in one sentence: A community library program in rural areas of Rajasthan has been providing free books and reading assistance to children from 150 villages since 2019. Volunteers visit schools every week to teach children to read and give them books to take home. A survey conducted three years later showed that the comprehension of children aged 8 to 12 improved by an average of two grade levels. The program has also increased regular school attendance by 18% in the participating villages.

Summary in one sentence: A consortium of engineering universities in South India has developed a low-cost water desalination unit powered entirely by solar energy. The unit, once installed, converts 1,000 litres of seawater per day into drinkable water without any fuel. Prototype tests conducted on the Tamil Nadu coast have shown that the unit can supply clean water to a village of 300 people at a lower cost than commercially bottled water. The team plans to release this design as open-source so that it can be replicated in other coastal areas around the world.

Write a summary in one sentence: A study conducted in Bangladesh, India and Nepal examined the effect of small-group micro-finance loans on women-dominated households. Women who received a $200 to $500 loan were significantly more likely to start or expand a small business within 18 months than those who did not receive a loan. The household income of borrowers increased by an average of 22 percent during the study period.

One of the main reasons for this is the overuse of antibiotics in human medicine and animal husbandry, which accelerates bacterial growth by applying constant selective pressure.

Pilot programs run in parts of Northeast India have found that students' school attendance has improved after gaining access to online learning materials.

From this location, the spacecraft can continuously observe the Sun without interruption. Its primary mission is to study the mechanisms behind the solar corona, solar wind and solar flares, which can disrupt satellite communications and power grids on Earth. India's Aditya-L1 spacecraft, which is installed in continuous view of the Sun at the Earth-Sun Lagrangian point, is designed to study solar corona activity and the causes of those flares that can disrupt Earth's communications infrastructure.

Summarize in one sentence: A large clinical study of 12,000 adults in six countries over ten years found that people who consumed more than 5 grams of salt per day had a 23% higher risk of developing high blood pressure than those who consumed less than 3 grams. The World Health Organization recommends consuming more than 5 grams of salt per day, yet surveys show that the average consumption in most countries is well above this limit.

Summarize the following information in one sentence: According to a study published by the International Centre for Integrated Mountain Development, glaciers in the Hindu Kush Himalayan region have been melting rapidly since the 1990s. The study, using four decades of satellite images, found that the amount of ice in the region has decreased by about 40% since 1980. Since these glaciers provide water to major rivers like the Indus, Ganges and Brahmaputra, their disappearance is threatening the drinking water supply for more than 20 million people living in low-lying plains.

The Coastal Monitoring Programme in the Lakshadweep group has recorded that 60% of coral reefs have been destroyed in the last two decades due to rising sea temperatures. Coral bleaching occurs when the water temperature rises a few degrees Celsius above the summer maximum level, causing corals to expel their nutrients and coloring algae. Without algae, corals starve and turn white and if temperatures remain high, they die. Reef loss has reduced the fish population in the area by an estimated 35%, affecting the livelihoods of local fishing communities.

Summarize the following passage in one sentence: India is one of the world's largest producers of barley, a drought-resistant grain grown in Maharashtra, Rajasthan and Karnataka. After decades of declining consumption due to widespread availability of rice and wheat through government distribution programmes, barley has regained importance due to its nutritional value and low water requirement. The United Nations in response to the Indian government's proposal declared 2023 as the 'International Year of Barley'.

Write a summary in one sentence: Oral rehydration therapy (ORS), which is a simple mixture of water, salt, and sugar, was developed in the 1960s in Dhaka, Bangladesh, after clinical trials demonstrated that it could effectively treat severe dehydration caused by hay fever and diarrhea. Prior to its widespread use, patients often needed to be given fluids through the veins, which had to be given to areas with limited resources. The World Health Organization estimates that oral rehydration therapy has saved more than 500 million medical lives since its inception.

India's National Solar Energy Programme, which was launched in 2010, had a target of installing 100 GW of solar capacity by 2022, which has been largely achieved. Rooftop solar panels and large-scale ground-mounted solar farms are now powering millions of homes and businesses across the country. Small solar-powered microgrids have delivered electricity to communities for the first time in remote villages without access to the national power grid. The cost of solar panels has fallen by more than 80% between 2010 and 2023, making the technology accessible even to low-income households.

Summarize the main argument in one sentence: Microfinance institutions provide small loans to individuals who do not have access to traditional banking services, and these typically target women from low-income communities. The Rural Bank of Bangladesh, founded in 1983 by economist Muhammad Yunus, formulated this model by lending small amounts to groups of women who collectively guaranteed repayment of each other. Studies in South Asia and sub-Saharan Africa have shown that microfinance recipients have higher rates of starting businesses and sending their children to school than non-recipients.

Summarize the following passage in one sentence: Smallpox was one of the deadliest diseases in human history, claiming an estimated 30 million lives in the twentieth century alone. India played a key role in the final phase of eradication, as its last major outbreak occurred in the state of Bihar in 1975. In 1980, the World Health Organization officially declared the eradication of smallpox, making it the only human disease to be eliminated through intentional vaccination.

Despite this protective role, India's mangrove cover has declined by about 40 per cent in the last four decades due to shrimp farming and coastal urban expansion.

Summarize in one sentence: India's Midday Meal Scheme, which was implemented nationally in 2001, provides free cooked meals to children enrolled in government primary and higher primary schools every school day. Summarize the key finding in one sentence: Microfinance programs initiated by rural banks in Bangladesh in the 1970s provide small loans without any guarantees to low-income borrowers, who are organized into groups that share social responsibility for repayment. Summarize in one sentence: In 2022, astronomers using the James Webb Space Telescope detected carbon dioxide in the atmosphere of the planet WASP-39b, located about 700 light-years from Earth. This discovery demonstrated that the telescope can accurately analyze the chemical composition of the atmospheres of distant planets, setting up a new observing instrument to study whether habitable conditions exist on distant planets. The Silk Road was a network of land and sea trade routes connecting China, Central Asia, Persia, and the Mediterranean world from about 200 BC to the 15th century AD. The four main causes of soil erosion in agricultural land are: 1. excessive use of chemicals and pesticides: this destroys the natural fertility of the soil and the activity of microbial nests. 2. deforestation: the removal of trees reduces the water storage capacity of the soil and leads to soil erosion. 3. irrigation: this leads to the loss of salinity in the soil and the continuation of crop rotations.

What is inflation and how does it affect the daily expenses of a typical middle-class family? Inflation refers to a general rise in the prices of goods and services, causing a decrease in the purchasing power of the currency. Explain the basic difference between a federal government and a unitary government. Also give a real-life example of both styles of government. In contrast, in a unitary government all powers are held by the central government and local governments operate only under the central government, as in the United Kingdom or France.

Explain to a curious twelve-year-old how a vaccine prepares our body's defense system to fight off disease. A vaccine is actually a 'hypothetical exercise' or training for our body's defense system. When our body sees this 'fake' attack, it immediately develops special weapons to fight it, called antibodies.

In areas where water is only supplied a few times a week, families often waste water or run out of supply. Early users of the device say they are now using their limited water reserves more carefully.

For many families, free meals are a big reason to send their children to school regularly. Teachers observed that students are more engaged and less likely to get sick at lunchtime. Summarize this in one sentence. One study found that free and nutritious school meals improve children's health, attendance, attention, and academic performance while also reducing dropout rates.

Summarize: Beekeepers in an agricultural area have observed that keeping honeycombs near orchards increases fruit production. As the bees move between flowers to collect juice, they transfer pollen, which results in flower fertilization. Farmers who allowed the beekeepers to come to their land increased their fruit harvest, while beekeepers received a steady source of honey.

Residents can now borrow from the library instead of buying items they occasionally need. This facility has proved particularly useful for low-income families and has encouraged a trend of sharing resources in the neighborhood. Summarize: A community library that provides books as well as tools, sewing machines, and sports equipment is helping residents, especially low-income families, to share resources with one another instead of buying rarely used items.

Summarize in one sentence: Astronomers have discovered a distant planet orbiting a star like our sun. The planet is located at a distance from its star where the temperature makes it possible for liquid water to exist on its surface.

Summarize in one sentence: A self-help group of women in a rural area set up a mutual fund by accumulating small monthly savings. Members can start small businesses or meet emergency needs by borrowing from this fund at low interest rates, avoiding high-interest lenders. Over the course of three years, several women have opened tailor shops and grocery stalls, and the group's savings have steadily increased.

Summarize in one sentence: A new mobile application allows farmers to take pictures of diseased plant leaves and get immediate advice on possible disease and treatment. It works by comparing the picture to a vast repository of plant diseases stored in the app. Farmers who do not have easy access to agronomists can now protect their crops by identifying skin diseases. A mobile app that identifies plant diseases from leaf images helps farmers without access to experts to diagnose problems early and save their crops.

Summarize the following text in one sentence: Health authorities have launched a vaccination campaign to protect cattle from a disease that spreads rapidly in cattle and can destroy farmers' herds. Teams are going to villages to provide free vaccines and also training farmers to recognize early symptoms of the disease. Since the beginning of the campaign, the spread of the disease has decreased and milk production in the area has stabilized.

In the first year, the farmers involved reported a 40% increase in crop production compared to those using conventional diesel pumps. The program aims to reach 2 lakh farmers within five years. The Government of India distributed free solar water pumps to drought-affected farmers, which already increased production by 40% in the first year and has now set a target of reaching 2 lakh farmers in the next five years.

Summarize the following paragraph in one sentence: Sub-Saharan Africa has seen a 30% decrease in malaria cases over the past decade, largely due to the widespread distribution of insecticide-laden mosquito nets. International health organizations have delivered more than 50 million mosquito nets to homes in high-risk areas. The greatest decrease in malaria deaths was seen in children, with a 40% decrease in under-five mortality.

Summarize in one sentence: Scientists who monitor Himalayan glaciers say that the volume of ice in the region has decreased by about 40 percent since 1970. The meltwater of the glaciers currently provides drinking water and irrigation facilities for some 20 million people in South Asia. As the glaciers shrink, the flow of rivers is likely to become irregular, threatening both agricultural and urban water supplies.

Summarize the following text in one sentence: A community library program in rural areas of Rajasthan has been providing free books and reading assistance to children in 150 villages since 2019. Volunteers visit schools every week, hold reading sessions, and give books to children to take home. A survey conducted three years later found that children aged 8 to 12 had an average two-fold improvement in reading comprehension. The program also increased school attendance rates in the respective villages by 18%.

Summarize the following text in one sentence: A consortium of engineering universities in South India has designed a low-cost solar-powered water desalination unit. The unit converts seawater into drinkable water without the use of any fuel after installation, with a capacity of 1,000 litres per day. Experiments conducted on the coast of Tamil Nadu have shown that the unit can provide clean water to a village of 300 people at a lower cost than commercial bottled water. The team plans to release the design as open source so that it can be developed in other coastal areas around the world.

Summarize the following paragraph in one sentence: A study spanning Bangladesh, India, and Nepal examined the effects of small group microfinance loans on women-led households. Women who received loans ranging from $200 to $500 were significantly more likely to start or grow a small business within 18 months than their non-borrowing peers. Borrowers' household incomes increased by an average of 22 percent during the study period.

Summarize in one sentence the following text: The Indian Ocean trade network, active from about the first century AD, connected traders from East Africa, the Arabian Peninsula, the Indo-Pacific and Southeast Asia. The monsoon winds were the primary technology that kept the network active: ships used the southwest monsoon to travel from Arabia to India each summer and the northeast monsoon to return each winter. The commodities exchanged included cotton cloth, spices, ivory and gold, making it one of the most economically important trading systems of the ancient world.

Summarize: A multi-year study conducted in six South Asian countries found that ground-level ozone pollution  caused by interaction with sunlight from vehicle fumes and industrial emissions  reduces wheat and rice production by 5% to 15% depending on regional pollution levels. This decrease in production occurs because ozone enters the leaves of crops through small vents and affects the photosynthesis process. Given current emission trends, researchers estimate that damage to crops from ozone will increase significantly by the middle of the century, increasing the pressure on food production in already high-risk areas.

Summarize in one sentence: In August 2018, Kerala experienced the worst floods of a century after twice the normal monsoon rains. More than one million people were displaced and about 483 people were killed in the disaster. Following the disaster, the Kerala government revised the method of releasing water from reservoirs and invested in early warning systems to mitigate the effects of future floods.

Summarize the following text in one sentence: The World Health Organization has warned that, if current trends continue, antibiotic-resistant infections could cause one million deaths per year by 2050. This resistance arises when bacteria develop defences against drugs that were previously effective in eliminating them. A major reason for this is the overuse of antibiotics in human medicine and livestock breeding, which accelerates bacterial evolution by constant selective pressures.

Over time, sunlight and waves cause the plastic to break down into tiny particles called microplastics, which are found in fish meat, drinking water, and seabird blood. A 2024 study confirmed the presence of microplastics at the bottom of the Mariana Trench, the deepest part of the earth, indicating that no part of the ocean is safe from them.

A study using satellite gravity data found that 30% of the world's major water bodies are falling at a faster rate than they are being replenished by rainfall. The water bodies below the Punjab and Haryana in northern India are among the fastest depleting in the world, due to decades of rice and wheat cultivation requiring enormous amounts of irrigation. Satellite data shows that 30% of the world's major water bodies are being depleted at a faster rate than they are being replenished by rainfall.

Summarize in one sentence: India's installed solar power capacity has surpassed 80 GW in 2024, making it one of the top five solar power producing countries in the world. This growth is due to falling prices of solar panels, government incentives for rooftop installations, and large-scale utility projects based in the Rajasthan desert. However, grid integration and battery storage are still major challenges as solar power production peaks at noon while domestic electricity demand is highest.

Summarize the following paragraph in one sentence: The human gut contains trillions of microbes collectively called the gut microbiome, which aid in digestion, control immune responses, and affect mood. A 2023 medical study found that participants who ate daily fermented foods such as yeast, fermented rice, and pickles had significant diversity in their gut bacteria after ten weeks.

Summarize the following text in one sentence: Satellite internet services in low-Earth orbit have begun to provide connectivity in remote villages in India where laying fiber optic cables is unprofitable due to hilly terrain or low population. Pilot programs in parts of northeast India have shown that school attendance of students has improved after gaining access to online educational materials. Indian telecommunications regulators are finalizing spectrum usage and pricing rules before approving wide-scale commercial launches.

Summarize the following text in one sentence: India launched its first solar observatory, Aditya-L1, in September 2023, which is placed in a halo orbit around the first Lagrangian point between Earth and the Sun. From this position, the spacecraft can continuously observe the Sun without interference from the Earth or Moon. Its primary mission is to study the mechanisms behind the solar corona, solar winds, and solar flares, which can disrupt satellite communications and power grids on Earth.

Field trials in the flood-affected districts of Bihar and Assam have shown that farmers using this new variety have achieved 80% of their expected harvest despite severe monsoon floods. The variety has been named 'Sorna Sub-1' and is now being distributed through government seed programs. Indian researchers have developed a flood-resistant variety of Dhol tea that is still being stored and distributed to farmers in the flood-affected state after three weeks in the water.

Summarize the following sentence: A large 10-year medical study of 12,000 adults in six countries found that people who consumed more than five grams of salt daily had a twenty-three percent higher risk of high blood pressure than those who consumed less than three grams. The World Health Organization recommends less than five grams of salt, but surveys show that the average salt intake in most countries is much higher.

Summarize the following text in one sentence: According to a study by the International Center for Integrated Mountain Development, the glaciers of the Indochinese Himalayas have been melting rapidly since the 1990's. Using four decades of satellite imagery, the study found that the ice volume in the region has decreased by about 40 percent since 1980. Because these glaciers supply water to major rivers, including the Indus, Ganges, and Brahmaputra, their disappearance threatens the drinking water supply for more than 20 million people living in low-lying areas.

Summarize the following quote in one sentence: The Coastal Monitoring Program in the Lakshadweep Islands has recorded that over the past two decades, 60 percent of coral reefs have disappeared due to rising sea temperatures. Coral bleaching occurs when the water temperature rises just one degree Celsius above the summer maximum for several weeks, causing corals to release the algae that provide them with color and nutrients. Without these algae, coral populations starve and turn white; if the temperatures remain high, they become extinct.

Summarize the following paragraph in one sentence: India is one of the largest producers of coarse grain crops (mullet) such as barley and barley, which are grown in Maharashtra, Rajasthan and Karnataka. After decades of declining consumption after the availability of rice and wheat, it is now being given renewed importance due to its nutritional importance and low water requirement. 2023 was declared 'International Millet Year' by the United Nations at India's suggestion, and now the government is encouraging farmers to return to its cultivation through subsidies and price support.

Summarize in one sentence: Oral rehydration therapy, a simple mixture of water, salt and sugar, was developed in the 1960s in Dhaka, Bangladesh, after clinical trials that proved it to be an effective treatment for severe dehydration caused by hemorrhoids and diarrheal diseases. Before its widespread use, patients often needed intravenous fluids, which were difficult to administer in low-resource areas. The World Health Organization estimates that oral rehydration therapy has saved more than 500 million lives since its introduction.

Summarize the following sentence: Mangrove forests are found in coastal areas of tropical regions, including the Sundarbans Delta in India and Bangladesh, which is the world's largest mangrove forest. These trees are capable of storing five times more carbon per hectare than tropical rainforests, making them important for climate balance. Mangroves protect the coastline from hurricanes and provide a breeding ground for commercially important fish.

Summarize the following text in one sentence: India's National Solar Energy Programme, launched in 2010, set a target of installing 100 GW of solar capacity by 2022, which has been largely achieved. Rooftop solar panels and large ground-based solar farms are now powering millions of homes and businesses across the country. Small solar microgrides have powered communities for the first time in remote villages where there was no access to the national power grid.

Explain the theme of the following quote in one sentence: Microfinance institutions provide small loans to individuals who do not have access to traditional banking facilities, and they generally target women from low-income communities. Bangladesh's Gramin Bank, founded in 1983 by economist Muhammad Yunus, pioneered a model by lending small amounts to groups of women who collectively guaranteed each other's repayment. Research in South Asia and sub-Saharan Africa has shown that those who receive microfinance have higher rates of starting a business and sending their children to school than those who do not.

Summarize the following paragraph in one sentence: Smallpox was one of the deadliest diseases in human history, claiming an estimated 30 million lives in the twentieth century alone. The strategy of 'color vaccination' was adopted during a global campaign led by the World Health Organization from 1967 to 1980, aimed at preventing the spread of the disease by vaccinating people around the infected. India played a key role in the final phase of the eradication of the disease, as its last major outbreak occurred in the spring of 1975.

Summarize the following paragraph in one sentence: Kerala, a state in South India, had achieved a literacy rate of over 96% by 2011, the highest of any state in India and better than that of several middle-income countries. The state's tradition of public investment in education dates back to the nineteenth century, when local rulers established schools and supported education that was then unprecedented in the region. Kerala's literacy campaigns are studied internationally as a model of how targeted government programs can rapidly increase access to education.

The spacecraft was built and launched at a cost of about US$450 million, which is significantly less than other space agencies' missions of the same type. The MarsYana was equipped with five scientific instruments aimed at studying the atmosphere, surface properties and mineral composition of Mars, and provided almost eight years of data until its first contact in 2022. The MarsYana was India's first successful Mars orbiter, reaching Mars in its first attempt in 2014.

Summarize the following paragraph in one sentence: Farmers in several states of India have adopted the rice intensification system, which uses organic fertilizer, less seed and less water instead of chemical fertilizers. Experiments in Maharashtra, Bihar and Tamil Nadu have shown that this method increases yield by 20 to 50% compared to conventional farming. The method requires no new equipment and can be adopted at no additional cost, making it highly suitable for smallholder farmers with little capital.

Summarize the following quote in one sentence: Oral rehydration therapy is a simple mixture of water, salt and sugar that was developed in the 1960s in Bangladesh and India after a measles epidemic. It enables the small intestine to absorb water even during severe diarrhea. The World Health Organization estimates that its widespread use has saved more than 500 million lives, mostly children under five years of age.

A study on Cyclone Amphan found that coastal villages that had mangrove forests suffered 30 percent less flood damage than villages where the forests had been cleared. Despite this protective role, mangrove forests in India have declined by about 40 percent over the past four decades due to shrimp farming and coastal urban expansion.

Summarize the following paragraph in one sentence: Digital payments transactions increased rapidly after India's demonetization policy in 2016, under which large-value notes were phased out of circulation. The number of monthly digital transactions increased from about 50 crore at the end of 2016 to over 9 billion by 2023. A major reason for this increase was the 'Unified Payments Interface' (UPI), an integrated system developed by the government that enabled instant transfers between different banks and applications and brought its use to rural areas.

Summarize the following passage in one sentence: The Silk Road was not a single road but a network of land and sea trade routes that linked East Asia, South Asia, Central Asia, the Middle East, and Europe for more than a thousand years. Merchants carried silk, spices, cotton, and precious stones, while scholars and missionaries used the same routes to spread religions, medical knowledge, and agricultural techniques across continents.

India's Midday Meal Scheme, which was implemented nationwide in 2001, provides free cooked meals to children enrolled in government primary and upper primary schools every school day. The program reaches more than 10 crore children and has been linked to an increase in school enrollment rates and a decrease in disconnection rates, especially for girls and children from low-income households. Research also shows that in the districts where the program is running on a regular basis, there has been a slight decrease in classroom hunger and child malnutrition.

Summarize the following text in one sentence: Microfinance programs, originated by Bangladesh's Gramin Bank in the 1970s, provide small, unsecured loans to low-income borrowers who are organized into groups that share a social responsibility to pay. This structure has kept the unemployment rate in many programs below two percent. However, decades of independent reviews have consistently shown that access to microfinance balances household spending during income shocks, but rarely leads to the large and sustained income increases claimed by early adopters.

Summarize in one sentence: In 2022, astronomers using the James Webb Space Telescope discovered carbon dioxide in the atmosphere of the planet WASP-39b, located about 700 light-years from Earth. This was the first clear identification of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere of a planet outside our solar system. The discovery proved that the telescope is accurately analyzing the chemical composition of the atmosphere of distant planets, providing a new observing tool to find out whether distant planets are habitable or whether the James Webb Space Telescope detected carbon dioxide in the atmosphere of an outer planet for the first time in 2022, confirming its ability to analyze the chemical composition of distant planets.

Within a decade, wheat production in the Punjab region alone had tripled, and by the end of the decade India was self-sufficient in grain. This change is largely credited to agronomist Norman Borlaug and Indian scientist M. S. Swami Nathan. In the 1960's and 1970's, India's 'green revolutions' increased the country's food production to three times that of its neighbors by producing more crops, increasing water resources, and improving food self-sufficiency.

Summarize the following statement in one sentence: During sleep, the brain repeats and organizes information acquired throughout the day, which researchers call 'memory consolidation.' Brain imaging studies show that the hippocampus, which is central to the formation of new memories, is highly active during specific stages of sleep. Volunteers who fell asleep after learning a new skill performed significantly better on memory tests the next morning than those who stayed awake.

Summarize the following quote in one sentence: The Sundarbans, which stretches along the coastal delta of Bangladesh and the Indian state of West Bengal, is the world's largest continuous mangrove forest. The forest acts as a natural barrier, absorbing waves during storms and protecting millions of people living in the delta. It is also one of the last habitats of the Bengal tiger, estimated to number between 100 and 130 in the Indian part alone.

Summarize the following passage in one sentence: The Silk Road was a network of land and sea trade routes that connected China, Central Asia, Iran, and the Mediterranean world from about 200 B.C. to the 15th century C.E. Merchants carried silk, spices, glass, and cotton for thousands of kilometers, although they rarely made the entire route themselves and goods were delivered through intermediary merchants. In addition to material goods, these routes helped spread Islam from India to East Asia, Buddhism to Southeast Asia, and epidemics such as the plague spread rapidly across the ancient continents.
